title: MEL Extension Definitions
description: Describes MEL language extension namespaces (format, log, ldap, secret, ...) and the functions they provide. Loaded by MelExtensionRegistry to drive semantic analysis and code completion for MEL expressions.

namespaces.description: Map of namespace identifier (e.g. 'format', 'log') to its definition. Key is the prefix used in MEL, e.g. format.strftime(...).

name.description: Function name as called in MEL, e.g. 'strftime' (used as 'format.strftime(...)' or, for dual-mode functions, 'value.strftime(...)').

receiverTypes.description: MEL types this function can be called on as a member function (e.g. ['timestamp']). Empty array means the function is namespace-only and has no member-call form. A non-empty set (including a future '*' wildcard) makes the function 'dual-mode': callable both as namespace.fn(...) and as receiver.fn(...).

parameters.description: Declared parameters in call order. For dual-mode functions this includes the receiver as the first parameter (matching the namespace-call form), e.g. strftime(value: timestamp, format: string).

returnType.description: MEL type of the function's result, e.g. 'string', 'timestamp', 'bytes', 'any'.

variadic.description: True if the function accepts a variable number of trailing arguments beyond those listed in 'parameters' (e.g. log functions accepting extra format arguments).

name.description: Parameter name, used for documentation/completion only (MEL extension calls are positional).

type.description: MEL type of the parameter, e.g. 'string', 'list', 'timestamp', 'any'.
